facade_axis_note: West facade: local x = -world y (local x increasing = world southward); local y = world z (up positive, ground z=0)

scale_origin.note: local origin = NW outer corner at ground (world x=0, world y=20, z=0); local x increases southward so world y = 20 - x_local; facade spans local x 0..20 (world y 20..0), z 0..4.5

strokes.note: F1 wall body (single floor, z 0.00-4.50); window openings overlay this fill
F1 window 1 (1500mm wide x 1800mm tall; sill z=1.00, head z=2.80; from bottom chain 540+1500 → x=0.54-2.04; height from left chain 1000+1800)
F1 window 2 (1200mm wide x 1800mm tall; sill z=1.00, head z=2.80; from bottom chain 540+1500+2380=4420 → x=4.42-5.62)
F1 window 3 (1500mm wide x 1800mm tall; sill z=1.00, head z=2.80; from bottom chain 540+1500+2380+1200+1740=7360 → x=7.36-8.86)
F1 window 4 (1500mm wide x 1800mm tall; sill z=1.00, head z=2.80; from bottom chain 7360+1500+1220=10080 → x=10.08-11.58)
F1 large window (4800mm wide x 2400mm tall; sill z=1.00, head z=3.40; from bottom chain 10080+1500+3080=14660 → x=14.66-19.46; height from right chain 1000+2400)

dimensions.note: bottom sub-chain: north margin (local x=0 is NW corner)
bottom sub-chain: pier between win1 and win2
bottom sub-chain: pier between win2 and win3
bottom sub-chain: pier between win3 and win4
bottom sub-chain: pier between win4 and large window
left chain: floor to window sill
left chain: window head to roof
right chain: floor to large window sill
right chain: large window head to roof

unknowns_noted: outline not drawn as a separate stroke: coincides with wall_fill outer edges (flat roof, single floor) → redundant, not traced
no door symbols visible on west elevation; all 5 openings are windows
win1-4 use left z-chain (sill=1.00, head=2.80, height=1800); large win5 uses right z-chain (sill=1.00, head=3.40, height=2400); the two window groups have different heights
uncaptured_visual_elements: outline silhouette coincides with wall_fill edges (flat roof, single floor), not drawn separately
